title: Creating Strong Passwords
drDigitalIntro: A strong password is your first defense against hackers. Strong passwords are long (12+ characters), mix uppercase and lowercase letters, include numbers and symbols, and aren't common words like 'password' or '123456'. Weak passwords are easy to guess — like your pet's name or your birthday. The longer and more random, the better. Never reuse the same password across multiple sites.

playgroundTask.instructions: Sort these passwords into Strong or Weak.

drDigitalSuccess: Strong passwords are long, random, and include symbols — weak ones are short and easy to guess. Now you know the difference!
drDigitalHint: Short, common words and simple numbers are weak. Long, random strings with symbols are strong.
